source.name: Phoenix event source registry
source.attribution: Official source pages and configured public event feeds

events.title: 243 Field Trip Attendees
events.description: Welcome!

events.title: Polka Dot Collage
events.description: Be inspired by Yayoi Kusama as you make a “Polka Dot Collage!”

events.title: Thing-a-ma-jigs
events.description: Using recyclables plus a dash of imagination, construct a one-of-a-kind masterpiece.

events.title: Visit the Garden!
events.description: Come get curious in the Children’s Garden with our Garden Specialist! Ask all your nature questions, help us water the plants, discovery new colorful blooms, and learn what’s alive out there. Who knows, maybe you’ll spot a new creature while you explore!

events.title: Weaving Discovery Station
events.description: It’s May-ker Month at the Children’s Museum! Drop by the Star Bar on the 2nd floor and dive into hands-on, tactile fun at our pop-up Weaving Discovery Station! Explore different weaving techniques, experiment with looms, braid and unravel, lace up geoboards, and even weave colorful designs onto metal sphere structures. What will you create? May-ker Month is

events.title: Corn Salsa with Edible Ed!
events.description: Sessions at 10:30am & 11:30am Join Edible Ed in the KIDchen, while we celebrate National Salsa Month! We’ll roast corn, chop veggies and mix up a dish you’ll be excited to dip into! Admission into this program is free with paid Museum admission. Space is limited. Please register at admissions upon arrival; first come, first serve.

events.title: Tree Bolts
events.description: Ever think to make art out of the bolts and washers and nuts you have in your home? Now you can! Join us as we use simple hardware to create trees and other designs. What will you try and make?

events.title: Linear Creations
events.description: Make art with string! Using cardboard shapes and colorful string, participants will explore patterns, shapes, and repetition to create unique designs. This hands‑on program encourages creative exploration and playful problem‑solving.

events.title: Psyko Steve Presents PRINCE DADDY & THE HYENA with REMO DRIVE, RESTRAINING ORDER & CELEBRATION GUNS with Remo Drive, Res
events.description: Psyko Steve Presents PRINCE DADDY & THE HYENA with REMO DRIVE, RESTRAINING ORDER & CELEBRATION GUNS with Remo Drive, Restraining Order, Celebration Guns Fri May 15 Prince Daddy & The Hyena Doors at 7:00PM / Show at 8:00PM at The Rebel Lounge All Ages , $25.00 Emo

events.title: Curious Curves
events.description: Explore the world of art in 3 dimensions. Design, build and create various sculptures with wire. What unique creations can you come up with?

events.title: Visit the Garden!
events.description: Come get curious in the Children’s Garden with our Garden Specialist! Ask all your nature questions, help us water the plants, discovery new colorful blooms, and learn what’s alive out there. Who knows, maybe you’ll spot a new creature while you explore!

events.title: Members-Only Extended Play Hours
events.description: Members-Only Extended Play Hours take place on the third Friday of each month, from 4pm-7pm. Join today and begin receiving benefits like this, and so much more right away!

events.title: Foam Zone Opens!
events.description: Bounce around in bubbles galore when the Foam Zone officially opens for the season on Saturday, May 16th! With the Foam Zone outside AND three floors and 300+ play experiences inside, a cool day of play is guaranteed! Foam play is free with paid Museum admission of $19.00 per person. Museum members and children under the age of 1 are free. FOAM ZONE HOURS: F

events.title: Aimee Little
events.description: We’re excited to welcome Aimee Little from YouTube’s Miss Aimee Littles to the Children’s Museum this Saturday! Come to our JLP classroom for a meet-and-greet with Aimee. You’ll get to say hello, play a little and even get a picture. It’s not every day you get to see your favorite YouTube educator in real life!

events.title: The Accidental Crafter
events.description: Are you a reluctant or inexperienced crafter? Are you looking for new projects but don't know where to start? Join us for an hour of creativity and fun as we all craft together! events.title: Try-It Tuesdays: Tinkering Around
events.description: Join our Weekly STEAM exploration program to explore STEAM concepts within our Monthly theme. This week we will try to find out what is ‘inside’ all of these technological devices! What really is inside a computer? A Toaster? A phone? Tinker around and see what you find!

events.title: “Mindful Me”: A Family Yoga & Mindfulness Class Presented by PLAY
events.description: Come discover mindfulness skills that you and your child can learn and share to create happiness, comfort and fun through art and yoga classes put on by PLAY (Play and Learn through Art and Yoga). events.title: “Arts & Crackers” with Edible Ed!
events.description: Sessions & 10:30am & 11:30am It’s May-ker Month! Let’s celebrate by building and designing delicious arts & crafts! We’ll use cookie cutters to create different shapes of cheese and build our own “arts and crackers” with spreads and fruity treats! Admission into this program is free with paid Museum admission. events.title: Bryson Bost Art
events.description: Artist and teaching artist Bryson Bost creates abstract, intuitive artwork that encourages curiosity, creativity, and hands‑on exploration. events.title: Sunflower Thai Noodles with Edible Ed!
events.description: Sessions at 10:30am & 11:30am Celebrate Asian American Native Hawaiian Pacific Islander month with a delicious treat! Taking inspiration from his Thai Peanut Noodle recipe, Edible Ed will show us how to mix up our very own sunflower Thai sauce, to enjoy over scrumptious noodles! Admission into this program is free with paid Museum admission. Space is limited

events.title: Hula Performance with Kuliaikapono Hula Studio
events.description: Join us as we celebrate Asian American Native Hawaiian Pacific Islander Month with music & hula! Kūliaikapono Hula Studio will perform hula kahiko (traditional) and hula ʻauana (modern), sharing with you the spirit of aloha! Admission into this program is free with paid Museum admission.

events.description: The Museum is open from 5pm-9pm free of charge, on the first Friday of each month. Space is very limited, and guests will be admitted on a first-come, first-served basis. Museum admission is not guaranteed due to extremely limited capacity. First Free Friday Nights at the Children’s Museum of Phoenix would not be possible without the generous support of:
